figures.caption: Fig. 1. Pipeline of the proposed framework. Synthetic patients are generated by conditioning a Large Language Model (LLM) on sampled personas and clinical knowledge retrieved via Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). Outputs are tabular datasets evaluated for fidelity and privacy against baselines. Abbreviations: LLM, Large Language Model; RAG, Retrieval-Augmented Generation.
TABLE I FIDELITY WITH 95% BOOTSTRAP CONFIDENCE INTERVALS. BEST RESULTS IN BOLD.
TABLE II IMPROVEMENTS OVER NO KNOWLEDGE BASE (NO-KB) WITH PAIRED 95% CONFIDENCE INTERVALS (CIS). POSITIVE VALUES INDICATE GAINS FROM DSM-V, ICD-10, OR DUAL-KB RETRIEVAL. BEST PER METRIC IN BOLD.
TABLE III PRIVACY METRICS. BEST PER METRIC WITHIN EACH DISORDER IS BOLD.
